channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Industry News
channel.description: Latest Industry News news in Context Engineering

item.title: Minimal, Local, Causal Explanations for Jailbreak Success in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.00123v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safety trained large language models (LLMs) can often be induced to answer harmful requests through jailbreak prompts. Because we lack a robust understanding of why LLMs are susceptible to jailbreaks, future frontier models operating more autonomously...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, release, prompt, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgentFloor: How Far Up the tool use Ladder Can Small Open-Weight Models Go?
item.description: arXiv:2605.00334v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Production agentic systems make many model calls per user request, and most of those calls are short, structured, and routine. This raises a practical routing question that existing evaluations do not directly answer: which parts of an agent workflow ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> instruction, release, GPT, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: excel-mcp-server - A Model Context Protocol server for Excel file manipulation
item.description: A Model Context Protocol server for Excel file manipulation<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.title: mcp-agent - Build effective agents using Model Context Protocol and simple workflow patterns
